parsed_review.summary: CP00-791 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, risk C, 150 units, RP26.P01.M06.S05-RP26.P02.M02.S22) is a descriptor-only fixture/review/service-foundation pack and passes hardened read-only review with no blocking findings. The pack-level claim is honest and bounded: manifest production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false; only the descriptor-foundation flag (enterprise_saas_fixture_review_service_foundation_descriptor_verified) is asserted, with production_ready_descriptor_verified=false pending this review and human adjudication. Deliverable distribution (impl 77 / ui 23 / fixture 4 / test 20 / hermes_evidence 4 / claude_review 7 / contract 3 / security_audit 6 / failure_recovery 6 = 150) and micro-title distribution match the prompt baseline exactly and are enforced in code by validateCoverage (packages/enterprise/src/validators.js:58) plus the model.test.js coverage assertions; all 14 local validation commands recorded in command-evidence.json exit 0 (npm test, validate, weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00 control-plane, git diff --check). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are strongly enforced via ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:120) and the SSO/SCIM validators that reject runtime, secret, or payload drift; command-evidence reports no_real_data=true and writes_product_state=false. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_packet.read_only=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false) and Hermes patterns are used only for descriptor-only evidence packets/receipts (emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write), not product runtime. The hardening contract is internally consistent (validation.valid=true, latest_pack/latest_projection=CP00-791) and the live plan cursor (CP00-791 -> CP00-792 / RP26.P02.M03.S01) is consistent across binding, handoff, and plan. Pre-closeout artifacts (construction-inspection, adjudication, claude-review-result) are in the expected PENDING state with empty valid_review_receipts; no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed, fenced, or tool-call-shaped output is presented as valid review evidence. blocks_goal_closeout=false because this pack explicitly disclaims runtime and enterprise-trust (claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true) and the human/enterprise-trust authority boundary is outside this receipt. One P3 informational note only.

findings.title: production_ready_flag label contains '_verified' suffix while verification booleans are correctly false pending review
findings.details: The pack target label production_ready_flag is the string 'enterprise_saas_fixture_review_service_foundation_descriptor_verified' (manifest.json:1541 and registry.js:115), whose '_verified' suffix could be misread as an achieved-state assertion. The actual verification booleans are correctly negative pre-review: manifest.production_ready=false (line 6), implementation_summary.production_ready_descriptor_verified=false (line 1563), and construction-inspection.json enterprise_saas_fixture_review_service_foundation_descriptor_verified=false (line 17). This is by design (the flag is a target identifier, not a current-state claim) and there is no over-claim, but it is recorded so the human adjudicator does not conflate the label name with verification status when promoting production_ready at closeout.
findings.recommendation: No code change required. At adjudication, treat the descriptor-verified flag as a target label and rely on the explicit boolean gates (production_ready_descriptor_verified, construction-inspection verdict) plus this review for promotion. Optionally, future pack generators could name the flag '<...>_descriptor_target' to remove the ambiguity.
